summary: fix(artistly): cache designs (records-path unwrap) and point offline search at the prompt-bearing scope so the searchable local archive works
reason: Two interlocking defects left the CLI's headline offline-search feature non-functional. (1) sync: both design resources return a two-sibling-array envelope {"designs":[...],"folders":[...],"hasMore":bool}. The generic item extractor (extractSingleObjectArraySibling) refuses to guess between two arrays, so it returned 0 items, the loop fell back to the single-object path, and UpsertDesigns got the whole wrapper (no top-level id) -> "missing id for designs" -> 0 designs cached. Fix: a deterministic resource->records-path map ({designs, designs-fetch-personal-designs} -> ["designs"]) unwraps the designs[] array before the heuristic result is trusted, with a hasMore-driven page-int continuation. (2) search: even with sync fixed, `search` queried the "designs" scope (from /designs-by-folder, a lightweight shape with NO positive_prompt); the prompt-rich records live under the "designs-fetch-personal-designs" scope (from /fetch-personal-designs). So prompt full-text matches never resolved. Fix: search the prompt-bearing scope. Masked in normal use because sync now exits 0 on non-critical resource errors (exit_policy_default_changed) and search returns [] with exit 0, so neither tripped polish; the publish full-matrix dogfood surfaced it.
validated_outcome: go build ./... and go vet ./... pass; internal/cli and internal/store test suites pass (incl. new TestExtractItemsByRecordsPath_Designs / _MissingKey and TestExtractDesignsPagination). End-to-end against the live API: `sync` caches 82 records (50 designs + 32 personal-designs) with errored:0; `designs list --data-source local` returns real IDs; `search "majestic"` -> 8 hits, `search "composition"` -> 26 hits (real prompt-text matches, previously []). Phase5 live gate still reports 6/89 failures, but those are the pre-existing dogfood harness fixture-resolution gap (literal <design-id> placeholder passed to designs download / edit upscale / prompt extract, which need a real resource and can't dry-run) — all three verified working with a real design id (exit 0, real output). That harness gap is independent of this change and already tracked by the artistly-refresh-example-ids patch.

deferred_to_upstream.feature: Generator response profiler should detect multi-array nested list envelopes ({"<resource>":[...],"<sibling>":[...],"hasMore":bool}) and emit a per-resource records-path, so sync need not be hand-patched per CLI.
deferred_to_upstream.reason: The press profiled these endpoints as flat/single-object responses with no records-path, producing a CLI whose sync silently cached the wrapper. Machine-level profiling gap; the local records-path map is the narrow CLI fix until the generator learns the pattern.

deferred_to_upstream.feature: When a resource is synced under multiple scopes with different field richness, the generator should point novel offline-search/list commands at the scope that carries the searchable fields (here, the full /fetch-personal-designs record rather than the lightweight /designs-by-folder shape).
deferred_to_upstream.reason: search was wired to the lightweight scope, so it found no prompt text even though prompts were synced under a sibling scope. The CLI-level scope fix is temporary until the generator picks the field-bearing scope for search.
